name: Mbombo
description: Mbombo, also known as Bumba, is the creator god in Kuba mythology. In the beginning, Mbombo existed alone in the darkness, suffering from a terrible stomach ache. After a long period of pain and discomfort, he vomited up the sun, moon, stars, and eventually, all living things. These included the first animals, plants, and humans. However, his creations were imperfect and prone to conflict. He then vomited up Bemba, the white heron, the first living creature. Bemba then vomited up a man named Nyonye Ngana, who in turn created white ants. These ants brought forth the earth, and from it grew all the other creatures and elements of the world. Mbombo, weary from his creation, retreated to the heavens, leaving his son, Woot, to rule over humanity and teach them civilization. Mbombo's act of creation is seen as both a divine act and a somewhat accidental occurrence, highlighting the chaotic and unpredictable nature of the universe's birth. He represents the raw, primordial force that birthed existence, even if it was born from discomfort and suffering. He is a distant but fundamental figure, a reminder of the universe's unpredictable origins. His role emphasizes the interconnectedness of all things, as everything originated from his being.
subtitle: The Vomiting Creator God

keyMyths.title: The Vomiting of Creation
keyMyths.description: Mbombo, suffering from a stomach ache, vomits up the sun, moon, stars, and life itself, creating the world. This act is the foundation of Kuba cosmology.
keyMyths.source: The Mythology of All Races, Volume VIII: Mythology of All Races: African and Oceanic

keyMyths.title: The Creation of Earth
keyMyths.description: Bemba, the white heron, vomits up Nyonye Ngana, who then creates white ants. These ants burrowed into Mbombo's vomit, bringing forth the earth and all its creatures.

keyMyths.title: Mbombo's Retreat
keyMyths.description: Exhausted after creation, Mbombo retreats to the heavens, leaving his son Woot to guide humanity and establish civilization on the newly formed earth.

sources.author: Werner, Alice
sources.description: Provides an overview of Bantu mythology, including the Kuba creation myth.
